result:
**Round 53 Goal: Phase Portrait Mode — plot two overlay scalar metrics against each other as a real-time 2D trajectory in the sidebar.**

The project already computes rich scalar summaries for each overlay (total entropy, Hamiltonian energy, magnetization, Lyapunov exponent, population, β₀ component count, correlation length ξ, etc.). A phase portrait plots one metric on X vs another on Y over time, tracing out the system's trajectory through a 2D parameter space. This reveals **attractor structure** — limit cycles, fixed points, bifurcations, chaotic wandering — that no single metric can show alone.

Toggle with `)`, cycle X/Y axis metric pairs with `<`/`>` in the mode. Render as a small Unicode scatter plot in the sidebar using Braille dots, with a trailing history of ~128 points fading from bright to dim. Display current (X,Y) values and axis labels.

This is pure exploitation: zero new analysis math, just combining existing scalar outputs into a powerful dynamical systems visualization.

Shall I build it?
